After clicking the orange-bordered magenta at (13,42):
- White-bordered moved to (13,42) but now has only 6 pixels (id=13, color=W, pixels=6) - it's smaller than before!
- Orange-bordered magenta moved from (13,42) to (11,41) (id=7, color=O, pixels=20)
- Gray-bordered at (9,49) with orange center (id=5)
- Gray-bordered at (11,34) with orange center (id=6)
- Gray-bordered at (35,45) with purple center (id=30)
- Purple-bordered magenta at (41,49) (id=34)
- Gray-bordered at (48,54) with purple center (id=40)
Interesting! The white-bordered now has only 6 pixels instead of 12. This suggests that clicking the magenta when white is at the junction point causes the white-bordered to shrink.
Wait, but previously white was at (20,44) which is the junction point. After clicking the magenta at (13,42), white moved to (13,42) and shrank to 6 pixels.
Let me think about what this means. The white-bordered object has 12 pixels when it's a full 3x3 border (or similar shape). With 6 pixels, it's a smaller border.
Maybe the goal is to shrink the white-bordered to a certain size? Or maybe the white-bordered needs to reach a certain position?
Let me continue clicking the magenta to see what happens. The orange-bordered magenta is now at (11,41), which is between (11,34) and (13,42).
If I click the magenta at (11,41), white would move to (11,41) and the magenta would move further along the trail.
But wait, the trail structure is:
- (9,49) ← (11,41) ← (13,42) ← (20,44) ← (11,34)
- (35,45) ← (20,44)
- (41,49) ← (48,54)
So (11,41) is between (9,49) and (13,42). If I click the magenta at (11,41), white would move to (11,41) and the magenta would move to (9,49), which is a gray-bordered position.
Let me try clicking the magenta at (11,41).
